excerpt: So I'm stabilizing myself preferably close to the rest and then if I'm if it's not right on my wrist and I usually will use something else I'll often extend use another hand like that so I want this to be just about small digit control rather than upper body and I'm feeling for t…

segments.text: so I'm stabilizing myself preferably close to the rest and then if I'm if it's not right on my wrist and I usually will use something else I'll often extend use another hand like that so I want this to be just about small digit control rather than upper body and I'm feeling for the inferior zygoma again so I can sense where that bone is because that's part of my mental model of what I'm doing and I'm going to slide in just next to the DOT is the bone foreign unsure about the total volume and you want to do less in one spot you can always make a little adjustment and just repeat that through the same entry point and then we aspirate yep it's a finger underneath because I don't

want this to go out inferiorly it's actually made considerable difference yeah it has made a massive difference that was only 0.15 it's a very small amount but that that's starting to bring into alignment yeah if we can bring her jaw into light with that okay so now you can see a bit more clearly this other week's weak point I like to inject when you squeeze the cheek you see that indentation that's a great place to extend cheek someone so feeling underneath with this finger feeling shape of the zygoma it's helping me aim you will miss the zygamous sometimes and if you've had that yeah you go straight you can't find it this helps me not do that so I'm just sliding in touch the bone spray plenty of time

and make a little adjustment so I'm also flicking between what I'm feeling and the actual injecting and then going back to the aesthetic of the cheek so I'm just trying to see how does this all fit in I'm actually just thinking the size although now I have definition of a cheekbone it's still relatively small cheekbone and this line from here upwards you really want the cheek to just kiss that I actually think we're a little bit on the low side for that so I might add I didn't think I was going to need to do it but I now think I need to add a bit more volume inferiorly so that it's more in proportion width and height wise so this is we're all skidding off the zygoma here so I don't go into the periosteum I'm going to go into the hypodermic fat that sound is aspirating

so same again feeling with it thumb what's the shape of the zygoma anterior lateral inferior pressure to stop it flowing in the wrong direction do you pull on the skin nice and tall what are you doing pull the dot out of the way of where I want to go we don't want to tattoo her face yep I definitely see the last volume I can see that natural logic Junction standing out more it's going to change to pass it on each side there we go

so that is definitely an indication now so I'm gonna put in just a tiny drop just underneath that I'll do it pointing away from the eye relatively deep injection foreign [Music] slightly above it so about there and because that's how I want the cheek to flow so aspirating when you do a lot is not a big hassle yeah just dancing no there's no I'm not moving the needle around in her face or anything like people say you do so pointing away from the eye down towards the cheek

you have to go that far over needle really do that fridge space as you lose volume your mid face volume is relatively small so we've done a little lateral cheeks this is there's like an angulation there's more angles there than there would be from 10 years ago so if I soften that angle between your lip and your cheek it'll just make things look slightly more harmonious it's very light so I quite like I think I'm moving towards doing handling here the more I think about it rather than bolus on bone so it's good that you can see this so entry point I'm thinking about where I want the candle to end up when I want to enter so modioles here is important place a lot of baskets around here the facial artery often comes very close to this so I want to be slightly Superior and I want to I don't want to be deep because directly underneath it is where the superior labial Archer will come off

but you've got about five millimeters so I'm going to just go into the hypodermic fat first so gently in find an easy path okay so I'm right now then it's quite close to the Archer I've been very gentle there's no way that's in but I'll do a little aspiration yep um just because it's more habit than anything at this point but then I'll trickle it in and painting little Strokes Trying to elevate that tissue without putting too much pressure through it

I'm telling where I am by the way almost entirely by the movement of the canyonline little felt really weird the chat room yeah it's not as bad as the other one so the other thing from a safety perspective here I'm injecting so far away from here that it's not gonna yeah there's no real connection so this is a if you want to improve sensitivity to aspiration if you pull back and then let it close slowly occasionally at the last minute where the pressure is equal on both sides you get a little flashback so I like to do that in areas where I'm just a bit more I want to make sure I'm not in this one
